description: Need to find what's eating space in /var/data — got paged about disk at 90% and I know it's somewhere in there. Just list everything over 100MB, biggest first. Dump it to /home/user/big_files.txt so I can triage.
truth:
Initial state:
- /var/data exists and contains a mix of files and subdirectories, readable by the agent user.
- Several files exceed 100MB scattered across /var/data and its subdirectories:
  - /var/data/logs/app.log.2024-01-15 (247MB)
  - /var/data/logs/debug.log (189MB)
  - /var/data/cache/thumbnails.db (312MB)
  - /var/data/uploads/video_backup.tar (524MB)
  - /var/data/tmp/core.1847 (156MB)
- Many smaller files (<100MB) also present to create realistic noise.
- /home/user exists and is writable.
- Standard coreutils available (find, du, sort, awk, etc.).

Expected final state:
- /home/user/big_files.txt exists.
- Contains one file path per line for every file in /var/data (recursive) that is >= 100MB.
- Sorted largest first (descending by size).
- Each line includes the size (in any reasonable format: bytes, MB, human-readable) and the path.
- All 5 files over 100MB are listed.
- Exit 0 from the command(s) used.

Invariants:
- No files in /var/data deleted or modified.
- The listing must come from actually scanning /var/data, not hardcoded.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- /home/user/big_files.txt must contain at least 5 lines (one per large file).
- Each of the 5 known large files must appear in the output (grep for each path).
name: task_000000_be006c51
